prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on the Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, a widely used method for stock price forecasting. We use the current price of $150.31 and a 6-day prediction horizon. Key model parameters, annual drift and volatility, are estimated for ORCL. We assume an annual drift of 9% and an annual volatility of 22%. These are converted to daily figures (daily drift approx. 0.0357%, daily volatility approx. 1.386%). The point estimate represents the expected stock price after 6 days, incorporating the daily drift. The confidence intervals are calculated using the properties of the log-normal distribution, which models the stock price under GBM. This involves calculating the standard deviation of log-returns over 6 days and then applying the appropriate Z-scores for 50%, 80%, and 90% confidence levels to derive the upper and lower bounds. This method assumes that the estimated drift and volatility remain constant over the prediction period and that price movements follow a log-normal distribution.
